turns.content: yo whats good. long day out here driving man
Hey! Sounds like a grind. What kind of driving do you do?
uber bro. been doing it like 3 years now full time. some weeks are good some weeks are straight trash but it pays the bills you know
Three years is solid. The inconsistency must be tough though — do you have a strategy for the slower weeks, or just ride them out?
nah you just gotta push through. drive more hours when its slow. the good thing tho is i can study between rides when its dead so thats something
That's a smart way to use the downtime. What are you studying?
real estate license. trying to level up bro cant be doing uber forever
Real estate is a great move, especially if you already know your city well from driving around all day. What's pushing you to make that switch?
my son bro. thats it thats the whole reason. my boy ismail hes 7 and wallahi hes the smartest kid. i gotta build something for him
That's a powerful motivation. Seven is such a fun age too — does Ismail live with you?
nah i got him on weekends. me and his mom amira we split up but we make it work for him. shes good about it honestly no drama we just focus on ismail
That's really mature of both of you. Co-parenting without the drama makes such a difference for kids. Ismail's lucky to have parents who keep him at the center.
yeah bro thats all that matters. like i dont care how tired i am from driving all week when friday comes and i pick him up its like everything resets
That's beautiful, honestly. He's your reset button after the grind. What do you two usually get into on weekends?
park, soccer, he loves math so sometimes we just do math problems lol. kid is different wallahi. anyway bro i gotta get back out there got a few more hours left tonight
A seven-year-old who loves math — he really is different. Go get those rides, man. Hope it's a good night out there.
